svg.text: 27. Security Overview
Your Application
(EC2 / Lambda / ECS with IAM Role)
Layer 1: IAM — Authorization (Who can access what?)
• Principle of Least Privilege: Grant only exact actions needed (e.g., dynamodb:GetItem, not *)
• Use IAM Roles (not hardcoded credentials) attached to Lambda / EC2 / ECS
• LeadingKeys condition for Row-Level Security (users see only their own data)
Layer 2: Encryption In Transit (TLS / HTTPS)
• ALL traffic between your app and DynamoDB is encrypted with TLS — enabled by default
• No manual configuration needed — AWS enforces HTTPS endpoint connections
Flow: App → (TLS encrypted tunnel) → DynamoDB — nobody can sniff packets
Layer 3: Encryption At Rest (KMS)
• All data stored on disk is AES-256 encrypted automatically via AWS KMS
• Default: AWS-managed key (free) | Advanced: Customer-managed CMK (more control)
putItem({ name: "Pradeep" }) → DynamoDB encrypts before writing to disk
Layer 4: VPC Endpoint (Private Network)
• Without VPC Endpoint: EC2 → Internet → DynamoDB (public route, needs NAT Gateway)
• With VPC Endpoint: EC2 → AWS private network → DynamoDB (no internet involved)
• Benefit: More secure, no NAT cost, traffic never leaves AWS backbone network
Layer 5: Client-Side Encryption (AWS DB Encryption SDK)
• Encrypt sensitive fields (PII) BEFORE data leaves your app — highest protection tier
• Flow: App encrypts with KMS key → sends ciphertext to DynamoDB (just an opaque blob)
• Even AWS cannot read your data — zero-trust approach for compliance (GDPR, HIPAA)
Security Layers (Defense in Depth):
1. IAM — Who can access (Authorization)
2. TLS — Data in motion protected
3. KMS at-rest encryption — Data on disk protected
4. VPC Endpoint — Private network (no public internet)
5. Client-Side Encryption — App-level encryption (even AWS can't read)
